corePrinciple.summary: Abnormalities are semi-public, but the truth is highly sealed.
corePrinciple.ordinaryPeopleKnow: Ordinary people know that strange events, urban legends, disappearances, cult crimes, shared dreams, sealed towns, unexplained bodies, and abnormal accidents exist. They do not know the real structure of Old Ones, corruption, dream contamination, forbidden knowledge, or unspeakable entities.
corePrinciple.officialPosition: Official institutions know more, but they do not publicly admit the abnormal truth. Public explanations usually frame abnormal events as disease, accident, crime, rumor, religious incident, mental illness, internet urban legend, natural disaster, terrorist incident, or experimental accident.

publicityLevels.name: fully_hidden
publicityLevels.label: Fully Hidden
publicityLevels.description: Ordinary people almost never know that abnormalities exist. Events are erased, contained, or disguised.

publicityLevels.name: occasional_rumors
publicityLevels.label: Occasional Rumors
publicityLevels.description: Occasional urban legends and strange news exist, but most people do not take them seriously.

publicityLevels.name: widespread_rumors_official_denial
publicityLevels.label: Widespread Rumors With Official Denial
publicityLevels.description: Strange rumors are common and civilian discussion is active, but official sources deny the abnormal truth. This is the default Cthulhu Life World level.

publicityLevels.name: public_knows_abnormalities_exist
publicityLevels.label: Public Knows Abnormalities Exist
publicityLevels.description: The public broadly knows that abnormalities exist, but still does not know the Old Ones, corruption, or the real structure of the world.

publicityLevels.name: official_abnormality_admission
publicityLevels.label: Official Abnormality Admission
publicityLevels.description: Official sources admit that abnormalities exist, but still hide the Old Ones, core pollution sources, and unspeakable entities.

publicityLevels.name: abnormality_public_order_changed
publicityLevels.label: Abnormality Public And Social Order Changed
publicityLevels.description: Abnormalities are public and have changed social order. The world approaches apocalypse, theocracy, containment society, or open conflict routes.

civilianKnowledgeClasses.label: Ordinary Majority
civilianKnowledgeClasses.knowledge: They know the world sometimes has strange events, but do not believe or do not want to believe in the abnormal truth.
typicalResponses: The world has plenty of strange things.
It is probably made up online.
Official sources already said it was an accident.
Do not spread rumors unless you want trouble.
civilianKnowledgeClasses.behavior: They keep studying, working, dating, buying homes, marrying, having children, and aging normally. They may fear strange news, but usually do not investigate.

civilianKnowledgeClasses.label: Sensitive Civilians
civilianKnowledgeClasses.knowledge: They believe the world is unclean or dangerous in ways normal explanations cannot cover, but they do not have a complete abnormal theory.
civilianKnowledgeClasses.behavior: They use experience, taboo language, folk practice, religion, and intuition to avoid danger.

civilianKnowledgeClasses.label: Conspiracy And Creepypasta Circles
civilianKnowledgeClasses.knowledge: They collect many mixed fragments. A small amount may be true.

civilianKnowledgeClasses.label: Contactees
civilianKnowledgeClasses.knowledge: They know some things truly exist and know officials are hiding something, but they do not understand the full truth.
examples: polluted but not fully collapsed people
routePressure: return to normal life at any cost

civilianKnowledgeClasses.label: Truth Insiders
civilianKnowledgeClasses.knowledge: They know that the surface world is false, but still may not know the full truth.
civilianKnowledgeClasses.principle: Nobody truly understands the entire truth. Official institutions manage disasters more than they understand them.

officialPolicy.summary: Do not publish, do not admit, do not spread, and do not let ordinary society collapse. Explain when possible, seal when needed, exploit when useful, isolate when unsolved.

officialFactions.label: Stability-Maintenance Faction
officialFactions.belief: It is merciful for ordinary people not to know the truth.
methods: make informed people disappear when necessary

officialFactions.label: Research Faction
methods: try to control Old One power

officialFactions.label: Compromise Or Surrender Faction
officialFactions.belief: The Old Ones cannot be defeated; humanity can only submit or bargain.
methods: trade a few lives for majority stability
stop investigators from revealing the truth

officialFactions.label: Resistance Faction
methods: sacrifice themselves to preserve social normalcy

incidentHandlingFlow: local police, hospital, or media first handles it as an ordinary incident
officials decide whether memory handling, containment, elimination, or recruitment is needed
publicAnnouncementTemplates: police are investigating; do not spread unverified information
aiRules: Ordinary NPCs may know strange rumors, but must not automatically know the Old One truth.
Official NPC knowledge depends on department, faction, clearance, and corruption level.
Visible abnormal events should first be wrapped by the social explanation library unless the publicity level has already risen.
Publicity level may change by region, era, event scale, and player character action.
The player character may believe, deny, exploit, reveal, cover up, or escape the abnormal truth.
